Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(layout-generation): implement algorithmic floorplan layout generation system

Adds a new feature module that generates multiple interior partition layouts
from a user-drawn boundary using squarified treemap subdivision with
architectural constraints. Phase 1 of the Finch 3D-inspired system.

Key components:
- Boundary extraction via half-edge face enumeration on planar graph
- Two-pass squarified treemap (zones → rooms) with area normalization
- WallGraph conversion with T-junction handling and node deduplication
- Door placement on shared walls using adjacency matrix weights
- Constraint validation (min areas, connectivity, window access)
- Corridor insertion for disconnected room graphs
- Scoring system (adjacency satisfaction, space efficiency, proportionality)
- Room program editor UI with dwelling type presets (1BR/2BR/3BR)
- Side-by-side variant card display with SVG mini-previews
- Zustand store for generation state
- Toolbar integration with layout generator panel toggle

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add cycle length range support with /cyclelength command

- Add cycle_length_min and cycle_length_max fields to User schema
- New /cyclelength command to view and update cycle length range
- Onboarding now captures cycle length range as final step
- Reminders fire across the full range window (e.g. days 28-34)
- Phase calculations use midpoint of range as effective cycle length

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add CVE-2026-3304 lab environment (AI-analyzed)

Docker-based reproduction environment for CVE-2026-3304, a DoS
vulnerability in Multer < 2.1.0 caused by incomplete cleanup of
orphaned temporary files on malformed multipart requests.

- Vulnerable app: Multer 2.0.2 (port 3000)
- Patched app: Multer 2.1.0 (port 3001)
- PoC exploit: Python script with HTTP/HTTPS support
- Monitoring: real-time orphaned file tracker

Analyzed using NVD API + GitHub Advisory + Claude AI.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: enforce timeout for async operations when server is unreachable (#3303)

AsyncMilvusClient operations hung indefinitely when the target server
was down because ensure_channel_ready() called channel_ready() with no
timeout, and the retry decorator could not enforce the user's timeout on
blocking calls. Fix both layers:

- Wrap each async retry attempt with asyncio.wait_for(remaining) so no
single attempt blocks beyond the user's timeout budget
- Add default 10s timeout to ensure_channel_ready(), matching the sync
_wait_for_channel_ready() behavior

See also: #3301

Signed-off-by: yangxuan <xuan.yang@zilliz.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

IQ/EQ testing, RevenueCat subscriptions, and dark theme design system.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add event bus system (v1.5.0 1/5)

Implement pub/sub event bus for cross-widget communication:

- EventBus: manage subscribers by event type
- Priority-based dispatch (higher priority = earlier callback)
- Type-safe event system with Event.type + optional data
- Subscribe/unsubscribe operations with ID tracking
- Bulk unsubscribe (unsubscribeAll)
- Query functions: subscriberCount, hasSubscribers, eventTypes
- 15 comprehensive tests (550/552 tests passing, 2 TTY-dependent skipped)

API design:
- Subscriber callback: fn(ctx: ?*anyopaque, event: Event) void
- Priority int (higher = earlier): allows fine-grained control
- Context pointer for stateful callbacks
- Memory-safe: caller owns event data lifetime

Next: v1.5.0 item 2/5 — Command pattern (undo/redo)

commit.message:
Add Paper Trading Engine to trading-brain

Autonomous paper trader that fetches real market data (CoinGecko + Yahoo
Finance), calculates technical indicators (RSI, MACD, trend, volatility),
makes entry/exit decisions, and feeds outcomes to tradeService.recordOutcome()
so the Brain learns from every paper trade.

New files (10): types, indicators, price-fetcher, portfolio-manager,
decision-engine, paper-engine, paper.service, migration, repository, CLI

Features:
- 5-minute autonomous cycle with real prices
- Watchlist: 10 crypto + 10 stocks, configurable
- Entry: RSI<30+MACD>0, trendScore>2+vol<60, confidence≥0.60
- Exit: SL -3%, TP +6%, trailing stop 4%/2%, time exit 48h
- IPC: 7 paper.* methods, 3 MCP tools, CLI commands
- Dashboard: Paper Trading section
- Starting balance: $10,000 USDT

commit.message:
Replace prescriptive design review rules with skill-based workflow (#54)

* Fix hero gradient and header contrast from design review

Hero (HighImpact):
- Replace full-screen linear gradient with centered radial vignette
  that only darkens behind text area, keeping image edges bright
- Add per-character text shadows for universal readability
- Shorten bottom feather from h-40/100% to h-16/80% opacity

Header:
- Increase non-scrolled background from 80% to 95% opacity
- Add shadow-sm in non-scrolled state for subtle ribbon effect
- Upgrade backdrop blur from sm to md for stronger frosted glass
- Full border visibility in both scroll states

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address review: Tailwind arbitrary value for vignette, seamless feather

- Replace inline style with Tailwind bg-[radial-gradient(...)] class
- Bottom feather now ends at full to-background opacity with
  via-background/80 midpoint for smooth blend without visible seam

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix hero bottom gradient: tall gradual fade replaces abrupt feather

The h-16 (64px) feather created a visible hard line where the
gradient stopped — it was the first thing the eye noticed on load.

Fix: h-48 (192px) with via-background/20 midpoint creates an
exponential opacity curve — first half is nearly invisible, second
half gently accelerates to match the page background. The transition
should be imperceptible.

Also updates skill-visual-design-review.md to correct the rules
that originally caused the too-short feather (the "max 4rem" and
"short feather" guidance was wrong and led to the abrupt line).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Replace prescriptive design review rules with principle-based skill pointer

The old skill-visual-design-review.md had specific CSS recipes (h-48,
via-background/20, drop-shadow values) that led to bad results. Replaced
with a lightweight pointer to the new /visual-design-review skill (v3)
which produces brand-anchored critique + fix strategies + implementation
tasks. Updated pre-PR checklist to reference the skill workflow.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address review: 6-viewport count, skill invocation path, scrim exception

- Fix viewport count from 5 to 6 (include 430px per repo standard)
- Use /visual-design-review invocation path instead of ~/.claude/ path
- Clarify rgba scrim exception for image overlays/shadows
- Replace .../pulls placeholder with {owner}/{repo}/pulls template
- Specify all 6 viewport widths in pre-PR checklist

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Extract hero scrims to utility classes, deduplicate header scroll styles

- Define hero-vignette, hero-text-shadow, hero-text-shadow-sm utilities
  in globals.css so HighImpact hero has no inline RGBA values
- Deduplicate header scrolled/unscrolled classes — shared styles extracted,
  only shadow-sm vs shadow-md differs per state
- Addresses CodeRabbit and Copilot review findings on PR #54

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Avoid equipment conflicts between Joost and Ruud on Monday

- D1: Pallof Press → Ab Machine (Joost uses cable station)
- D2: Leg Raise → Plank (Joost uses captain's chair)

commit.message:
fix: P0 round 2 — stage ledger, guaranteed finalization, mandatory final.json

- Stage execution ledger (StageEntry) tracks all stages (success/failed/skipped)
- compute_job_status() derives status from ledger (single source of truth)
- FinalizeReportStage runs via try/finally (guaranteed even on failure)
- final.json always written by ExportStage; minimal version for failed jobs
- Dynamic artifacts map (checks files on disk, not hardcoded)
- exc.attempts_used set at all retry exit points for accurate feedback
- Duplicate job_id validation in run_batch()
- Unique job_id for CLI single command (timestamp+uuid)
- batch_report saved per batch_id + latest copy
- 15 new tests (189 total, all passing)

Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)
via [Happy](https://happy.engineering)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add MK bone-region analysis and ART interaction ANOVA scripts

New scripts for MK morphological analysis across Sex x Treatment x Bone:

- mk_interaction_analysis.py: Proper Aligned Rank Transform ANOVA
  (Wobbrock et al. 2011) using statsmodels Type II SS. Supports
  --score-threshold for flexible filtering. Generates interaction
  plots and ANOVA summary heatmaps.

- mk_comprehensive_analysis.py: Full pairwise Mann-Whitney, Kruskal-Wallis,
  and hand-rolled ART across all factor combinations.

- split_detections_by_bone.py, annotate_bone_regions.py,
  calculate_tissue_areas.py, compare_tissue_vs_bone_outlines.py:
  Data preparation pipeline for bone-region assignment and tissue
  area quantification.

Key finding: sex-dimorphic response to HU treatment — females show
+30% MK area and +21% density, males show +7.5% area and -25% density
(crossover interaction). Proper ART alignment reveals interactions
masked by the old approach (n_cells Sex*Trt: old p=0.58, new p=0.028).

commit.message:
fix: add CSP nonce support to allow SvelteKit inline scripts

The app showed a blank white screen because the CSP header blocked
SvelteKit-generated inline scripts. Instead of using unsafe-inline,
generate a per-request cryptographic nonce and inject it into both
the CSP header and HTML script tags.

Also fix go:embed directive to use all:dist so that SvelteKit _app/
directory is included in the binary.

commit.message:
fix: add wildcard cert domains to Traefik routers (SecOps pattern)

Patch step 12d adds tls.domains[0].main/<sans> labels to every HTTPS
router in the NetBird compose, requesting a wildcard cert for *.base-domain
via the Cloudflare DNS challenge.  Mirrors the SecOps/Traefikv3 approach
(tls.domains[0].main=dwgroup.au + sans=*.dwgroup.au) so a single cert
covers netbird.dwgroup.au and any other subdomains.

Without this, Traefik attempted per-hostname certs but the ACME storage
was shared with a stale cert, causing cert errors on netbird.dwgroup.au.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(#295): Add directory tree to selector prefetch context (#297)

* feat(#295): Add directory tree to selector prefetch context

Implements GitHub Issue #295: セレクタエージェントのファイルパス推測ミスを防止する（ディレクトリツリー提供 + list_directory 先行指示）

## Changes

- Added `_build_directory_tree()` function to `_prefetch.py` that generates a lightweight flat path listing of project source files (max 500 entries, 5000 chars)
- Added `directory_tree` field to `PrefetchedContext` model to store the generated tree
- Updated `_build_prefetched_section()` in `_instruction.py` to include Directory Tree section in prefetched context
- Added directory tree guardrail to `_prefetch_guardrail()` in `_selector.py` to instruct selector agent to always verify file paths against the tree
- Added File Navigation guidance to `selector.toml` system_prompt, emphasizing list_directory tool usage
- Added comprehensive tests (15 new tests across 3 test files)

## Key Features

- Scans `src/` directory if present, otherwise scans CWD
- Excludes common build/cache directories (__pycache__, .git, node_modules, .venv, etc.)
- Excludes compiled files (.pyc, .pyo)
- Sorted output for predictable listing
- Graceful truncation when limits exceeded
- All 1947 tests pass with 100% quality (ruff, mypy)

commit.message:
Replace LRU/MRU/FIFO/LFU with SIEVE eviction (#20)

* Replace LRU/MRU/FIFO/LFU with SIEVE eviction across both backends

Unify the eviction algorithm to SIEVE for both the in-process memory
backend and the cross-process shared memory backend. This simplifies
the codebase (removes ~540 lines), eliminates the `strategy` parameter
and `Strategy` enum, and makes shared memory reads fully lock-free
(visited bit is set via an idempotent store, no write lock needed).

Key changes:
- Shared memory: replace multi-strategy dispatch with sieve_evict()
- Layout VERSION bumped 2→3 (forces recreation of old mmap files)
- All reads on shared backend are now lock-free (previously only FIFO)
- Remove src/strategies/ module entirely (memory backend uses inline SIEVE)
- Remove Strategy enum from Python API; cache() no longer takes strategy=

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update documentation for SIEVE eviction

Replace all references to LRU/MRU/FIFO/LFU strategy selection with
SIEVE eviction across README, usage guide, performance docs, alternatives
comparison, benchmarks, llms.txt, examples, and CLAUDE.md.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update performance docs with fresh SIEVE benchmark data

Run benchmarks after SIEVE migration to capture improvements:
- Shared backend: 7.8M → 8.9M ops/s (lock-free reads via visited bit)
- Multi-process 4 procs: 3.1M → 7.7M ops/s (2.5x improvement)
- Multi-process 8 procs: 1.9M → 6.5M ops/s (3.4x improvement)
- Hit rate: 64.9% → 72.7% (SIEVE scan-resistant eviction)

Also fix bench_runner SharedCachedFunction calls (removed old strategy arg).
